title: with_key\! macro drop-sentinel pattern is an invisible contributor trap
description: The with_key\! macro prevents HSM cache slot leaks by clearing key.id to KeyId::ERASED before eviction, suppressing the Drop impl's warning. This only works correctly if: (1) the key type's field is named 'id', (2) the field has type KeyId, and (3) the Drop impl checks 'self.id \!= KeyId::ERASED'. All six current key types follow this pattern. A contributor adding a new key type (e.g. DH or RSA-PSS) will copy one of the existing modules but may name the field differently, use a different sentinel, or write the Drop check differently. The eviction will still work, but the leak-detection warning will either never fire or always fire, and the failure mode is silent in production. The invariant is not expressed in the type system or in any comment on the macro itself.
